title: Referências
text:
ANSELIN, Luc. Spatial data
science. In: RICHARDSON, Douglas (Org.). The
International Encyclopedia of Geography: People, the Earth, Environment,
and Technology. Hoboken, NJ: John Wiley & Sons, 2019.


ARONOFF, Stan. Geographical Information Systems: A Management
Perspective. Ottawa: WDL Publications, 1989.


BRADY, Shailaja R.; SINHA, A. Krishna; GUNDERSEN, Linda C.
Geoinformatics 2007: data to knowledge: Scientific
Investigations Report. Reston, VA: U.S. Geological Survey, 2007.
Disponível em: <https://doi.org/10.3133/sir20075199>.


BRODEUR, Jean. Standardization in Geomatics: in Canada and in ISO/TC
211. Geomatica, v. 55, n. 1, p. 91–106, 2001.


BURROUGH, Peter A. Principles of Geographical Information
Systems for Land Resources Assessment. Oxford, England: Oxford
University Press, 1986.


BURROUGH, Peter A.; MCDONNELL, Rachael A. Principles of
Geographical Information Systems. Oxford: Oxford University
Press, 1998.


CÂMARA, Gilberto; DAVIS, Clodoveu; MONTEIRO, Antônio Miguel Vieira.
Introdução
à ciência da
geoinformação. São José dos
Campos: INPE, 2001. p. 344


CÂMARA, Gilberto; MONTEIRO, Antonio Miguel Vieira. Conceitos basicos em
ciência da Geoinformação.
In: CÂMARA, Gilberto; DAVIS, Clodoveu; MONTEIRO, Antônio Miguel
Vieira (Orgs.). Introdução
à ciência da
geoinformação. São
José dos Campos: INPE, 2001. p. 35.


CÂMARA, Gilberto; QUEIROZ, Gilberto Ribeiro de. Arquitetura de Sistemas
de Informação Geográfica. In: CÂMARA, Gilberto; DAVIS,
Clodoveu; MONTEIRO, Antônio Miguel Vieira (Orgs.). Introdução à
Ciência da Geoinformação. São José dos Campos: INPE, 2001. p.
35.


CLARKE, Keith C.; PARKS, Bradley O.; CRANE, Michael P.
Geographic Information Systems and Environmental
Modeling. 1. ed. New Delhi: Prentice-Hall of India Pvt. Ltd.,
2006.


GEWIN, Virginia. Mapping
opportunities. Nature, v. 427, n. 6972, p. 376–377,
2004.


GOODCHILD, Michael F. Geographical information science.
International journal of geographical information
systems, v. 6, n. 1, p. 31–45, 1992.


HU, Yingjie. Starting with data: advancing spatial data science
by building and sharing high-quality datasets., 2020.
Disponível em: <https://arxiv.org/abs/2007.08087>


KRAWCZYK, Artur. Proposal
of Redefinition of the Terms Geomatics and Geoinformatics on the Basis
of Terminological Postulates. ISPRS International Journal of
Geo-Information, v. 11, n. 11, 2022.


LONGLEY, Paul A. et al. Geographic Information Science
and Systems. 4. ed. Chichester: Wiley, 2015.


NATIONAL RESEARCH COUNCIL. What
Is GIScience? In: Learning to Think Spatially: GIS
as a Support System in the K-12 Curriculum. Washington, DC:
National Academies Press, 2006.


OLĘDZKI, Jan Romuald; KWIATKOWSKA, Joanna M. Geoinformatics-an
integrated spatial research tool. Miscellanea Geographica.
Regional Studies on Development, v. 11, p. 323–331, 2004.


ROBERTS, A. F. Geotechnology:
An Introductory Text for Students and Engineers.
[S.l.]: Pergamon Press, 1977.


SCHEIDER, Simon et al. Why geographic data science is
not a science. Geography Compass, v. 14, n. 11, p.
e12537, 2020.


TOMLINSON, Roger F. An Introduction to the Geo-Information
System of the Canada Land Inventory. Ottawa, ON, Canada:
Agricultural; Rural Development Administration, 1967.


WATERS, Nigel. GIS: History.
In: RICHARDSON, Douglas et al. (Orgs.). The
International Encyclopedia of Geography: People, the Earth, Environment
and Technology. Chichester: Wiley, 2017.
